title: Phase 7: Streaming
description: Implement real-time streaming via both SSE (Server-Sent Events) and WebSocket. Clients differ on which they use — both are required. The infrastructure is a tokio broadcast channel keyed by (account_id, channel). Five channel types: user, public, public:local, hashtag, hashtag:local, direct. Four event types: update, notification, delete, status.update.
design: tokio::sync::broadcast per channel type. SSE: text/event-stream, Connection: keep-alive. WebSocket: axum-ws. Event format matches Mastodon wire protocol exactly (type field + payload field). Channel keys: user:{account_id}, public, public:local, hashtag:{tag}, hashtag:local:{tag}, direct:{account_id}. Broadcast on post write, delete, notification creation.
acceptance_criteria: Phanpy receives live update events when a new post arrives on the home timeline; delete event fires when a post is deleted; notification event fires on mention; both SSE and WebSocket endpoints work; client can subscribe to multiple channels
